title: Podman images --format '{{json .}}' output incompatible with docker
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I labelled this as a bug, even though I am not sure if docker compatibility is even a feature.

When running `docker images some-image:some-tag --format '{{ json . }}'` the output is nicely split across lines.

However in docker the output is in a single line per image.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run `docker images some-image:some-tag --format '{{ json . }}'` 

2. Output is nicely formatted and not in one line, which breaks tool that assume it to be in one like (e.g. ansible-test https://github.com/ansible/ansible/blob/devel/test/lib/ansible_test/_internal/docker_util.py#L312)

**Describe the results you received:**

A multi-line nicely formatted json output per image.

**Describe the results you expected:**

A single line json output per image as returned by docker.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.5
Built:        Tue Dec  8 15:37:50 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**


No - I did not install the version from master, as the packaged one seems the newest released
Yes - I did check the Troubleshooting Guide

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Easy fix would to make this human friendly.  If stdout is a tty then break it on lines, if not then output without the line feeds.

@rhatdan I actually realized that just stripping newlines won't fix it, as Podman does actually output a valid JSON list in this instance, whereas docker just output a few lines of JSON objects:

Podman:
```json
[{ <some_values>}, { <some_values> }]
```

Docker:
```json
{ <some_values }
{ <some_values }
```

I actually think I just got the wrong impression that the `podman-docker` package would try to provide `docker-compatible` output, which I guess it doesn't (and honestly parsing output of a CLI invocation is sketchy at best and the upstream `ansible-test` already contains a special case for `podman`).

I'd be fine leaving the output as is and closing this issue, knowing that docker-compatibilty is not a goal for the `podman-docker` cli wrapper.
Well it is the goal, except where there are clearly bugs in Docker, like asking to output json and then Docker does not output valid json.
